text.title: Embedding Generation Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every embedding producer must satisfy. Each rule is enforced by validate-embedding-generation.py.

rule.statement: Producer MUST return byte-identical vectors for byte-identical inputs (same model id, same dimension, same preprocessing). No randomness is permitted in pre- or post-processing.
rule.rationale: Index re-runs must produce stable IDs; non-deterministic preprocessing causes duplicate vectors and breaks dedup.

rule.statement: Cache key is sha256(normalized_text) + ":" + model_id + ":" + dim. Cache value is the vector. Cache is byte-content addressed; never use a sequential ID or filename.
rule.rationale: Re-runs over the same corpus must hit the cache; key by ID lets renamed/moved content waste an API call.

rule.statement: For corpora ≥1000 chunks, producer uses async parallel batches sized to the provider limit (e.g. OpenAI 2048 inputs, Voyage 128). Concurrent batches respect provider rate limits via a semaphore.

rationale.source: OpenAI / Voyage / Cohere API docs 2026 batch limits

rule.rationale: Sequential 1-by-1 calls take 10-100× wall-clock and waste connection setup; over-parallel calls trip 429.

rule.statement: If the downstream consumer uses cosine similarity, producer L2-normalizes vectors before persisting. Once normalized, persistence flag normalize=true MUST be set in the producer config.
rule.rationale: Normalized vectors make cosine = dot product, simplifying indexes and eliminating runtime sqrt; mixing normalized and unnormalized vectors in one collection produces silently wrong rankings.

rule.statement: Vector store schema declares dimension EXACTLY; producer config asserts model_dim == schema_dim before any upsert. Switching model+dim requires a new collection, not an in-place override.
rule.rationale: Dimension mismatch is silent corruption in some stores and a hard error in others; either way the search is broken.

rule.statement: Every embedding API call is wrapped in retry-with-jitter (max_attempts=5, wait = min(2**attempt + jitter, 60)). Retry on 429, 5xx, and connection errors. Other 4xx fail fast.
rule.rationale: Long batch runs trip rate limits intermittently; crash-on-first-429 wastes minutes of partial work.
